title: Async Context, Logs, Metrics, Traces, Health, and Incident Evidence · Contract Lens: Slo Alert Incident
summary: The service logs secrets and raw paths, labels metrics by identifiers, loses trace context, reports healthy during saturation, and cannot connect an alert to a user outcome. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts slo alert incident increment in bounded telemetry and health evidence contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Connect service-level indicators and objectives to multi-window alerts, deploy and trace evidence, bounded incident diagnosis, mitigation, recovery, and learning.
Separate liveness, readiness, startup, dependency state, saturation, draining, and management endpoint protection without causing probe storms.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Slo Alert Incident · Theory Slo Alert Incident
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 3640 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to emit bounded route telemetry and distinguish server errors from readiness capacity; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Connect service-level indicators and objectives to multi-window alerts, deploy and trace evidence, bounded incident diagnosis, mitigation, recovery, and learning.
